title: Garlic Butter Shrimp
tagline: 15-minute dinner. Butter, garlic, shrimp. That's it. That's the recipe.
description: Juicy shrimp sautéed in garlicky butter sauce with lemon and parsley. Serve over pasta, rice, or with crusty bread.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs large shrimp, peeled and deveined
1/4 cup dry white wine (or chicken broth)
steps: Pat shrimp dry with paper towels. Season with salt and pepper.
Melt 2 Tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add shrimp in a single layer. Cook 1-2 minutes per side until pink. Remove to a plate.
Reduce heat to medium. Add remaining butter and garlic. Sauté 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in wine and lemon juice. Let bubble 1-2 minutes, scraping up any browned bits.
Return shrimp to the pan. Toss to coat in the sauce. Add red pepper flakes.
Remove from heat. Sprinkle with parsley and serve immediately.
notes: Don't overcook: Shrimp go from perfect to rubbery in seconds. Pull them when just pink.
Size matters: Large or jumbo shrimp work best. Small shrimp overcook too easily.
Dry the shrimp: Wet shrimp steam instead of sear. Pat them very dry.
Serve with: Angel hair pasta, rice, crusty bread, or over zucchini noodles.
No wine: Substitute chicken broth with a splash of extra lemon juice.
